req.title: Close encounter (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a state machine (open -> finished), a guard on further modifications to closed encounters, an addendum exception, and mandatory AuditEvent emission. Four tests are generated: (1) positive/EP: happy-path close sets status=finished and emits AuditEvent; (2) negative/NEG: modifying a closed encounter yields HTTP 409; (3) boundary/BVA: addendum Observation is the sole allowed write after close; (4) state-transition/STC: double-close attempt verifies idempotency or correct error on already-finished encounter. Each test creates its own encounter fixture to remain self-contained and idempotent.

tests.name: test_close_encounter_sets_finished_and_emits_audit
preconditions: A new Encounter exists with status=in-progress
actions: Query AuditEvent store for the close operation
expected: At least one AuditEvent exists referencing the Encounter id with action='close'
trace: R-HLT-082: clinician shall close an Encounter
R-HLT-082: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_encounter_sets_finished_and_emits_audit(
    api_client, create_encounter, clinician_token, audit_query
):
    enc_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress", token=clinician_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"

    enc = api_client.get(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    ).json()
    assert enc["status"] == "finished", f"status={enc['status']}"

    events = audit_query(resource_type="Encounter", resource_id=enc_id, action="close")
    assert len(events) >= 1, "No AuditEvent emitted for close operation"
    assert events[0]["resourceId"] == enc_id

tests.name: test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: A new Encounter exists and has been closed (status=finished)
actions: POST /Encounter/{id}/close to move encounter to finished
Attempt PATCH /Encounter/{id} with a non-addendum field update
expected: Response body contains an error indicating encounter is closed
AuditEvent is still emitted for the rejected modification attempt
trace: R-HLT-082: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications
R-HLT-082: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409(
    api_client, create_encounter, clinician_token, audit_query
):
    enc_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress", token=clinician_token)
    close_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200

    patch_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        json={"period": {"end": "2099-12-31"}},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409 for closed-encounter modification, got {patch_resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = patch_resp.json()
    assert "closed" in str(body).lower() or "finished" in str(body).lower()

    events = audit_query(resource_type="Encounter", resource_id=enc_id, action="update")
    assert len(events) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted even for rejected attempts"

tests.name: test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter
preconditions: A new Encounter exists and has been closed (status=finished)
actions: POST /Observation with encounter reference and note.extension indicating addendum
Verify Observation is persisted and AuditEvent is emitted
expected: Observation.encounter references the closed Encounter id
AuditEvent is emitted for the addendum write
trace: R-HLT-082: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications EXCEPT addendum Observations
R-HLT-082: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter(
    api_client, create_encounter, clinician_token, audit_query
):
    enc_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress", token=clinician_token)
    api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )

    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"text": "addendum"}],
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        "code": {"text": "Addendum note"},
    }
    obs_resp = api_client.post(
        "/Observation",
        json=obs_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert obs_resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Addendum Observation should be accepted, got {obs_resp.status_code}"
    )
    obs_id = obs_resp.json()["id"]

    events = audit_query(resource_type="Observation", resource_id=obs_id, action="create")
    assert len(events) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted for addendum Observation"

tests.name: test_double_close_encounter_state_transition
preconditions: A new Encounter exists with status=in-progress
expected: First POST returns HTTP 200 and status becomes finished
Second POST returns HTTP 409 or HTTP 422 (invalid state transition)
AuditEvent is emitted for both close attempts
trace: R-HLT-082: state transition open->finished is valid exactly once
R-HLT-082: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications
R-HLT-082: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_double_close_encounter_state_transition(
    api_client, create_encounter, clinician_token, audit_query
):
    enc_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress", token=clinician_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}

    first = api_client.post(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close", headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200, f"First close failed: {first.status_code}"

    enc = api_client.get(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", headers=headers).json()
    assert enc["status"] == "finished"

    second = api_client.post(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close", headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409/422 on double-close, got {second.status_code}"
    )

    events = audit_query(resource_type="Encounter", resource_id=enc_id, action="close")
    assert len(events) >= 2, (
        f"Expected AuditEvent for both close attempts, found {len(events)}"
    )
